operator_request: Keep Ferric Forager a native Rust rewrite/reimagination that cannot use yt-dlp as product behavior; first harden project Codex prevention rules, then address every finding with no more than six terra xhigh subagents in parallel.

research_reopen_triggers: A selected dependency, upstream oracle version, or source assumption is proven stale.
A remediation decision lacks evidence in current design, review, WP-003, WP-004, WP-005, or WP-012 sources.
existing_research_sources: The current technical design reference set and peer-review corpus
Validated WP-003 build and proof infrastructure
Validated WP-004 compatibility inventory and pinned-oracle corpus
Official Rust Clippy lint-configuration documentation for fully-qualified disallowed methods, types, and macros: https://doc.rust-lang.org/clippy/lint_configuration.html
selected_approach: Supersede the deficient v1 activation instead of backfilling its history.
Keep yt-dlp outside product crates, packaging, runtime, fallback, wrapper, and sidecar paths; allow only pinned non-shipped oracle generation and comparison.
Make proof reports distinguish static support declarations from evidence actually executed.

Combine product-local compiler-resolved Clippy forbids for raw process construction and byte/text embedding with the independent source gate required for include!, which the pinned Clippy release does not observe reliably.
Replace helper-predicate fixtures with isolated mutations and composed/public boundary execution.
Complete lifecycle effect outcome and recovery semantics, explicit schema-transition authority, and exact counterexample tests.
Retain the prerequisite zero-progress ceiling until a shipped Rust Ferric entrypoint consumes and re-proves the work.
rejected_options: Backfilling the v1 prerequisite declaration after activation.
Adding yt-dlp or Python as a product dependency, subprocess, fallback, wrapper, sidecar, installer payload, or runtime service.
Treating fixture hashes, declaration equality, toy predicates, empty shard selection, or test-name presence as behavioral proof.
Allowing peers to opt into arbitrary schema drift with booleans or an empty authority token.
Treating effect emission as effect completion or leaving failed/cancelled effects unrecoverable.
Repeating completed research without a named reopen trigger.
reuse_opportunities: Reuse pure Rust contract types, lifecycle transition engine, bounded graph model, strict Serde DTOs, and existing test crates.
Reuse the WP-004 corpus as provenance-bound inputs while upgrading replay selection and reporting.
Reuse xtask reporting, rule-to-proof mapping, temp-copy helpers, runtime truth, and adversarial review structure.
basis.validation_plan: Implement three non-overlapping remediation lanes, run focused and workspace tests, compile alias and embedded-asset bypass probes through product-local Clippy forbids plus the independent source gate, execute compatibility and canonical gates, execute isolated mutations against actual governed boundaries, inspect result-level proof metadata, rerun the exact public counterexamples with execution receipts, and fail closure if any evidence still depends only on declarations or local helper literals.

assumptions: Existing research and approved dependencies remain current.
yt-dlp is permitted only as a non-shipped compatibility oracle and is never Ferric product behavior.
No shipped Ferric entrypoint exists, so all closure remains prerequisite-only.
non_goals: Creating or claiming a Ferric downloader entrypoint.
Broad architecture redesign unrelated to reproduced failures.
New dependencies or broad external research beyond the targeted official Clippy verification triggered by a reproduced alias/include bypass.

minimum_controls: Product scans reject direct and indirect yt-dlp or Python coupling in manifests, code, assets, package inputs, process plans, and non-text documentation paths.
Every selected replay case executes; empty selection fails; every PASS identifies only the proof class actually executed.
Counterexamples mutate isolated governed inputs and invoke the composed/public boundary.
Every external effect has correlated acknowledgement, failure, cancellation, and recovery paths before durable or terminal state.
Schema compatibility names reviewed source-to-target pairs; legacy peer booleans grant no authority.
A required public test emits a validated execution receipt so a named empty body cannot pass.
plausible_failures: A future neutral wrapper embeds or invokes yt-dlp from a documentation exception path.
An empty shard or static proof map is reported as semantic or production-runtime evidence.
A mutation fixture validates only a local literal predicate.
A process cleanup or resource release fails and the machine has no legal recovery transition.
A V1 permissive flag appears to authorize unrelated schemas after migration.
A test remains listed and named while its body is empty.

execution_decomposition: Commit this correctly predeclared v2 activation checkpoint.
Delegate lifecycle, diagnostic, and compatibility/proof/reporting remediation to three non-overlapping terra xhigh lanes.
Integrate inventories, fixtures, rule mappings, and manual centrally.
Run canonical gates and fresh independent high-risk adversarial validation.
Commit, merge, verify main containment, and remote-back only after all acceptance surfaces pass.
